title: ID Agents Control Center Wiki
description: Human-readable wiki and editable UI schematic for the Control Center. Each page.body field is the prose wiki article; the desktop app still reads page nav metadata for sidebar labels, icons, order, and visibility, and keeps the structured sections as supporting implementation detail.

editContract.hotReload: The desktop app polls this file while running. Local edits to body prose, nav labels, icons, order, visibility, page descriptions, tabs, tiles, fields, dropdowns, controls, displays, and routing notes appear in the UI without a rebuild.
editContract.pathOverride: Set IDCTL_WIKI_PATH=/absolute/path/to/CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.json to drive the UI from a different local schematic file.
editContract.routeRule: A page only becomes navigable if its route is implemented in idctl-desktop/src/renderer/App.tsx. Unknown routes are shown in the Wiki but skipped by the sidebar.
editContract.body: pages[].body is markdown prose for operators. Keep each article grounded in current renderer behavior, explain when to use the page, and call out routing or safety boundaries. Do not remove nav, route, component, sourceFiles, or structured sections because those continue to drive the live UI and schematic detail.
editContract.releaseDrift: scripts/check-wiki.mjs compares this appVersion and updated marker with idctl-desktop/package.json, validates page sourceFiles, and fails when a page source file changes without docs/CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.json changing in the same worktree diff. Set IDCTL_WIKI_DRIFT_BASE=<ref> to compare committed branch changes against a base ref as well.
editContract.releaseNaming: Operator-facing release notes, ops/deployer handoffs, and ops-team content-ops release-cycle wiki updates should say IDACC release / idacc release, not IACC release / iacc release. IDACC means ID Agent Control Center.

global.shell: Electron desktop app. Renderer calls window.idagents.call(method, ...args); preload sends IPC to main; main either handles app-local methods or forwards manager methods through bridge.ts and ManagerClient.
global.state: useFleet in src/renderer/store.ts keeps the live snapshot: connection, manager URL, active team, coordinator, agents, teams, events, inbox, unread chat count, all-team agents, and refresh functions.

routing.routesInto: settings, chat, plans, projects, questions, dreams, loops, images, updater, subscriptions, Computer Use broker
wiki:get app-local IPC method, Wiki page, sidebar metadata

footer.display: Connection pill, manager URL, active running-agent count, running-team count, and offline errors.

toasts.display: Global progress/result/error notices for long-running operations.

promptModal.display: In-app prompt replacement for Electron where browser prompt is unavailable.

pages.purpose: Operator home: pick a running team, talk to that team's lead, watch cross-team coordination, and read the live activity feed.
pages.scope: All-team observation with a selected chat team. The embedded chat dispatches to the chosen team's lead; the activity feed merges events across teams.

fields.label: Chat composer
fields.description: Message body for the selected lead. Enter sends, Shift+Enter inserts a newline.

dropdowns.description: Selects the team whose lead receives the embedded chat.

dropdowns.label: Focus project
dropdowns.description: Optional project scope inside the embedded chat.

controls.label: Send
controls.description: Dispatches the composer text to the locked lead and starts query polling.

controls.label: paperclip
controls.description: Adds files to the chat session; files are copied into the project upload folder or agent workspace.

displays.label: Lead state

displays.label: Event rows
displays.description: Topic, team tag, human-readable description, and age.

notes: The older static product spec described a dashboard agent grid; in current source that fleet grid lives on Health through AgentTable.
pages.body:
# Dashboard

Dashboard is the daily command view. It combines lead chat for the selected team with all-team coordination, active task state, recent events, and hierarchy context so an operator can steer work without losing fleet awareness.

## Use it for

- Send a request to the selected team's lead while watching coordination across every running team.
- Check whether work is moving through the leadership chain before opening the deeper Work or Health pages.
- Read the event stream when you need fast context on what just changed.

## Watch for

The Dashboard is not the fleet roster grid. Agent lifecycle controls, probes, and full roster inspection live on Health through AgentTable. If the question is "who is running?" use Health; if the question is "what should the lead do next?" start here.

pages.purpose: Full chat workspace for sessions, agent targeting, project focus, file attachments, generated images, resumable dispatches, and live behind-the-scenes activity. NOTE: Chat is embedded into the Dashboard (lead-locked via the Chat `embedded`/`lockTarget` props) and is no longer a standalone nav page — the Dashboard is the lead chat.
pages.scope: Active team by default. Embedded callers can pin a specific team or lock a target.

tiles.label: Sessions
tiles.description: Saved chat list with unread dots, rename/delete, new chat, and session restore.

tiles.label: Messages
tiles.description: Conversation transcript with system, user, agent, file, image, pending, and persisted trace messages.

tiles.label: Behind the scenes
tiles.description: Live tool/file/delegation stream while an agent reply is running.

fields.label: Chat title
fields.description: Rename the open session.

fields.label: Composer
fields.description: Main message entry. It also routes image and plan-like requests through local heuristics.

dropdowns.label: Session picker

displays.label: Generated image
displays.description: Cached local image rendered through a data URL because file:// is blocked by CSP.

pages.body:
# Chat

Chat is the full conversation workspace behind Dashboard lead chat. It owns sessions, target selection, project focus, attachments, generated images, live traces, resumable polling, unread state, and saved history.

## Use it for

- Continue or inspect a conversation with complete message history.
- Attach files, paste context, generate image context, or focus the dispatch on a tracked project.
- Review the behind-the-scenes trace for tool, file, and delegation activity after a reply lands.

## Routing note

Chat is currently embedded into Dashboard and hidden from normal sidebar navigation. Dashboard uses the same dispatch path but locks the target to the selected team's coordinator; direct Chat use is for contextual or deep-link flows that need the full session surface.

pages.purpose: Operator decision queue: answer blockers surfaced by agents and manager inbox items waiting on a human reply.
pages.scope: Active team for blocker questions; manager inbox from store snapshot.

tiles.label: Decisions needed
tiles.description: Questions raised from Work blockers, sorted by task dependency depth.

fields.label: Manager reply
fields.description: Direct response to a pending manager inbox item. Cmd/Ctrl+Enter sends.

controls.label: Option buttons

controls.label: I'll handle it
controls.description: Tells the agent the user is taking ownership and clears the blocker.

controls.label: Skip
controls.description: Dismisses a blocker question without answering and clears any review overlay.

controls.label: Dismiss
controls.description: Clears a manager inbox item with a dismissed marker.

displays.label: Awaiting count
displays.description: Total of manager inbox items plus blocker questions.

pages.body:
# Inbox

Inbox is the operator decision queue. It separates direct blocker questions and manager inbox items from the general event feed so work that needs a human answer is visible and actionable.

## Use it for

- Answer option questions surfaced by agents or Work blocker scans.
- Reply to manager inbox items that are explicitly waiting on the operator.
- Dismiss stale prompts after deciding they no longer need a response.

## Operating rule

Use Inbox to resolve blockers, then return to Chat or Work for continued execution. It is intentionally narrower than a conversation view: the goal is to clear pending decisions and update the linked task state where the manager provides a task reference.

pages.body:
# Work

Work is the execution surface for plans, task cards, schedules, loops, and dream review. It turns operator intent into tracked manager work and gives the team a place to move items through planning, assignment, review, and completion.

## Start here when

- A request needs to become tasks, not just a chat reply.
- You need to triage unassigned work, assign a fleet objective, or surface blockers for a human decision.
- You want scheduled follow-up, recurring loops, or reviewed idea drafts.

## Tab guide

- Plans: structured plans and dispatch handoff.
- Tasks: Kanban execution, review lanes, scoped assignment, and blocker surfacing.
- Schedule: team-aware heartbeats and supervision reminders.
- Loops: recurring objectives.
- Dream: proposed ideas or simulations that require review before action.

Dragging cards changes the local review lane and syncs mapped manager status when appropriate. Assignment can dispatch to one team, selected teams, team leads, or every active agent, grouping manager plan creation by target team so owners stay scoped correctly. Schedule heartbeats use team-qualified targets, so cross-team agents and manager-level heartbeats remain visible and editable from the Schedule tab.

pages.body:
# Projects

Projects is the local workspace registry. It tracks folders and GitHub-backed repositories that agents can use as context, along with metadata that makes future dispatches safer and easier to aim.

## Use it for

- Add or import repositories before asking agents to work on them.
- Set descriptions, tags, branch context, and automation preferences that explain how a workspace should be handled.
- Review git status and prepare operator-approved git actions without turning the Control Center into an automatic committer.

## Good practice

Choose or update the project before sending code work through Chat or Work. A clear project focus reduces ambiguity for agents and makes attachments, uploads, and generated plans land in the expected workspace.

pages.body:
# Health

Health is the live fleet and usage view. It shows running state, token and model usage, provider activity, and the full agent roster through AgentTable.

## Use it for

- Confirm which agents are running, stopped, busy, or missing from the active fleet.
- Probe agent liveness, start or stop workers, rebuild where supported, and inspect team membership.
- Compare current token usage and model/runtime behavior before changing staffing or routing.

## Boundary

Health is optimized for operational status from the store snapshot and all-team aggregate poll. It is the current home for the roster grid that older specs placed on Dashboard, but it is not a long-term analytics warehouse.

pages.body:
# Identity & Keys

Identity & Keys is the review surface for agent identity, wallet posture, Safe-style account state, and scoped session keys. It helps operators understand who can act and what account or key material is associated with that action.

## Use it for

- Select an agent and inspect identity status, account details, session keys, and warnings.
- Review delegated capability posture before relying on an agent for identity-sensitive work.
- Keep onchain identity, OWS wallet, Safe account, and session-key concepts visible in one place.

## Safety note

Some provider behavior still depends on the active manager backend. Treat this page as the UI contract and verify the live provider before documenting stronger guarantees about signing, custody, non-expiring keys, or production-grade authorization.

pages.body:
# HR Manager

HR Manager is the organization design and routing workspace. It covers team structure, agent creation, roster management, instructions, and outbound relay topology.

## Owner

The legal-team `hr-manager` agent owns this page's staffing workflows, instruction-drafting behavior, and optimization proposals. Escalate legal-team policy or personnel-process questions through `legal/general-counsel`.

## Tab guide

- Structure: inspect the team graph and leadership shape.
- Build: draft and create agent rosters.
- Manage: edit deployed agents and their instructions.
- Route: inspect how teams delegate across the fleet.

## Operating rule

Changes here affect staffing, instructions, and delegation paths. Review generated drafts before saving, syncing, or rebuilding, and use the graph as a whole-fleet planning aid rather than a view of only the active team. Agent instruction editing is now team-qualified in the picker and save path, so selecting an agent from another team loads, saves, and rebuilds that exact team/agent instead of silently falling back to the active team.

pages.body:
# Capabilities

Capabilities manages the extension surface available to teams and agents: MCP servers, skills, and plugins. It answers whether the fleet has the tools and instructions needed for a request before work is assigned.

## Use it for

- Inspect installed MCP servers, skills, and bundled plugins.
- Attach or review capabilities for the selected team scope.
- Confirm tool availability before assigning work that depends on a connector, library-specific instruction, or specialist workflow.

## Scope note

Team scoping happens inside this page rather than only through the global footer selector. Keep interop fields explicit: MCP, OpenAPI, A2A, and signed-write discovery are separate concerns and should not be collapsed into one generic capability claim.

pages.purpose: Supervised local computer-control broker: bless capable agents, arm/disarm control, watch the screen, approve actions, and panic-stop.

displays.description: Shows whether the broker is armed, paused, or disarmed.

displays.label: Approval prompt
displays.description: Pending action with allow/deny controls and auto-decline behavior in broker.

pages.body:
# Computer Use

Computer Use is the supervised local desktop-control broker. It shows the live display stream, permission state, pending approval prompts, and recent computer-use activity for agents that are allowed to request screen or input actions.

## Use it for

- Bless capable agents, arm or disarm control, and supervise the live stage.
- Approve or reject pending screen/input actions.
- Use panic controls quickly when local control should stop.

## Safety boundary

Actions should run only through the armed broker and approval flow. The page depends on local OS permissions such as Screen Recording and Accessibility. Navigating away stops the live renderer view, although global notifications can still report relevant broker state.

pages.purpose: Infrastructure panel for host hardware, manager connection, coordinator, self-update, subscriptions, local models, image server, local stacks, and inference backends.

pages.body:
# Settings

Settings contains local desktop configuration for hardware, manager connection, coordinator defaults, self-update, subscriptions, Ollama, image services, local stacks, and inference backends.

## Use it for

- Fix manager connectivity or confirm the manager URL used by the app.
- Review subscription CLI state, local model providers, image generation services, and inference defaults.
- Check for, stage, and relaunch into app updates.

## Boundary

These settings are app-local configuration, not manager task data. Because release and update behavior is surfaced here, keep this page aligned with the package version and release process; the wiki drift check exists to catch stale release-facing documentation before shipping.

pages.purpose: Live documentation and editable UI schematic for the Control Center itself.
pages.scope: Reads this JSON file through app-local IPC. Does not touch manager state.

controls.label: Sidebar schema
controls.description: Changing pages[].nav.label/icon/order/visible updates the app sidebar for implemented routes.

displays.label: Source path
displays.description: Shows the loaded file path and modification time so operators know what file is driving the page.

pages.body:
# Wiki

Wiki is both the human documentation page and the editable schematic for the Control Center UI. Each article explains the operator purpose of a page; the structured sections below preserve tabs, tiles, fields, controls, displays, routes, and source files for implementation detail.

## Use it for

- Search pages, controls, routes, source files, and notes from inside the desktop app.
- Confirm how a UI surface is intended to behave before editing code or filing follow-up work.
- Keep app navigation metadata and human documentation in one reviewed JSON artifact.

## Editing rule

The desktop app hot-reload polls CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.json through the main-process wiki bridge. Sidebar labels, icons, order, and visibility still come from pages[].nav for implemented routes, so documentation edits must preserve that metadata and keep every page.body non-empty.
